sentences.text: Hello.

but winter time kind of thing up here, you know.

like pitched us with getting us like the lowest entry point of the software we could.

We could look at this up or we like the data that I'm able to get on my own versus what I could pull very quickly from your program, um, and like why that would be a benefit to us.
So I'm, I'm, uh, it's gonna be still a little bit longer of a process, I think.

So like being able to justify the cost and then try to like find other ways besides me just being able to use it that we could make like practical sense for it.

So, um.

um I know we discussed that briefly with Eric, I don't since he's not here, it's kind of not as relevant, but on the property management side, it's a huge time saver, um, and also making sure you're working with a reliable daily data.

with everything changes so fast.

the value, yeah, yeah, and I would agree too like I, since I don't work on that side of the business as much, um, like mine's more in the speculative nature, so that's why I think they're struggling with me coming up with the value myself.

rely upon, uh, it's gonna take me 10 amount of hours to do that same amount of work.

This is the amount of hours that I've spent.

OK.
I'm curious, is there a is there like a price that does make sense?

Oh man, I, I don't have that number in front of me, but I think part of what this next little trial will do is like as I'm kind of trying to determine how much time I'm spending and how much cost that's we're incurring in doing that, that that might kind of open up that window that they would say like.

To get approval.

For that extended trial period of time, I do see a lot of value in the tool, um, and, and especially like the ability of being able to like replicate previous research quickly.

um, it's something I'm asked to do often, so, um, I usually have to rely upon other people to help find that information and so that's part of where like if I were to be utilizing other people's time, I'm gonna try to document that as well and try to like really really build that um That case for why I think that we should try to proceed forward with that.
